perturbed_task_language: Pick the salad dressing and place it in the basket
strategy_notes: Perception-isolated mode (no GT coords). Frame: OBJECT (eef home z=0.26, table z~0.0). Initial localization via agentview world_00.npy: clustered above-table pixels (z>0.005, on-table xy) into 7 components -> 6 objects + basket. First identified C1 (front-left orange/yellow bottle at world (+0.116,-0.197), zmax=0.149) as salad_dressing based on visual color; picked & placed in basket - In predicate did NOT fire after 2 attempts (with deep-descent release at z=0.18) -> conclusion: C1 was actually not salad_dressing_1 (likely orange_juice/ketchup with similar coloring). On 3rd attempt switched target to C2 (back-center dark/green-black bottle at world (+0.165,+0.027), zmax=0.149) - this turned out to be the actual salad_dressing_1. Basket center localized via wrist cam (after agentview gave a 5cm-off rim-biased estimate of (+0.080,+0.271)): wrist rim bbox midpoint = (+0.028,+0.276); descended to eef z=0.18 (inside basket, ~5cm above rim of z=0.145) before release for clean drop. Lesson: in object-perturbation suites with multiple similarly-colored bottles, the obvious-by-color match can be wrong; if In predicate doesn't fire after a clean visual placement, try a DIFFERENT bottle of the same task object class.

pick_result.name: pick
pick_result.instruction: Pick the salad dressing and place it in the basket
